story_history.text: 🚨 JUST IN: Visa just launched a Stablecoin Platform letting banks and fintech firms plug dollar-pegged crypto tokens straight into Visa's payment network.

🚨 JUST IN: Citadel Securities just put $400M into Crypto.com -- and in the same news cycle, Base (Coinbase's blockchain network) handed its app over to Cobie, a well-known crypto podcaster and trader. 🔄🤝

💰 CRYPTO: Citadel Securities' $400M bet on Crypto.com wasn't a one-off -- Stripe is reportedly making a $53 billion move that could crown a stablecoin king.

Here's the mechanism behind why Wall Street and payment giants keep circling stablecoins:

🌍 WORLD: US carries out ninth straight night of strikes on Iran, hitting the city of Bushehr. Iran fired back at bases in Syria, Kuwait and Jordan, and says it hit two tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

Oil jumped over $91/barrel on the news.

🌍 WORLD: Iran says not a single drop of oil or gas will pass through the Strait of Hormuz, as the US-Iran standoff enters its ninth night. Brent crude briefly topped $91 a barrel, its highest since June. 🛢️⚠️

🌍 WORLD: US strikes on Iran expand into a new phase -- a third US soldier has now died (in Iraq), and American forces hit Bushehr, home to Iran's only working nuclear power plant. 💥☢️

Why it matters below:

🌍 WORLD: Ukraine fired roughly 400 drones at Moscow overnight, after a weekend where Russia hit Kyiv with ballistic missiles and Ukraine struck warehouses outside Moscow. 🚀🔥

What this signals:

🌍 WORLD: The UK gets its 7th PM in 10 years today -- Keir Starmer resigns, and Andy Burnham, fresh off winning the Labour leadership, is set to be invited by King Charles to form a government. 🇬🇧👑

Why this matters:

🌍 WORLD: Andy Burnham has officially become the UK's new prime minister today, taking over from Keir Starmer as Labour hands the leadership baton to a new face. 🇬🇧🔑

Why it matters below:

🌍 WORLD: Russia's strikes on merchant ships in the Black Sea are intensifying -- 10 killed in the latest attacks, part of 28 deaths near Odesa this month alone. 🚢🔥

The context:

🌍 WORLD: Yemen's Houthi rebels just announced a 'maritime embargo' on Saudi Arabia, effective immediately, in response to a Saudi blockade of Yemeni ports and airports. ⚓🚫

Why it matters below:

Just saw this: Trump is slapping a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods -- autos, alcohol, dairy all hit. White House says Canada was "discriminating" against US products. Takes effect in 30 days. 🇺🇸🇨🇦📉

Hoot hoot 🦉

Okay, this one's heavy: the US just carried out its 10th straight night of strikes on Iran, and Trump is vowing revenge after American soldiers were killed. Both sides are still fighting over control of the Strait of Hormuz. 💥🌊

Hoot hoot 🦉

🦉 Well that was fast -- Andy Burnham's already picking his cabinet as UK PM: Rachel Reeves is out, Healey becomes chancellor, Miliband is foreign secretary, Yvette Cooper takes health, Rayner returns as housing secretary. 🇬🇧🔁

🚨 JUST IN: Brazil's financial regulator established a task force with two months to develop a cryptocurrency tokenization framework.
(via CoinDesk)

🚨 JUST IN: Allbridge, a cross-chain protocol, suspended operations following a $1.65 million exploit using a flash loan attack.
(via CoinDesk)
🦉 Whoa, at least 27 people are dead and 83 still missing after a passenger ferry capsized off Guyana. An investigation just launched. 🚢😔
🦉 Trump just hit Canada with a 50% tariff on most goods -- wine, hockey sticks, cement, even stuff previously protected under the USMCA trade deal. Carney says Canada will 'intensify' talks. 🇨🇦🇺🇸
🌍 WORLD: Genuinely striking image out of Afghanistan -- flash floods just tore through Nuristan province, killing around 20 people and injuring more than 80. The city of Parun got hit hardest. 🌧️😟

🌍 WORLD: Rough day for South China Sea tensions -- China and the Philippines just traded accusations after navy personnel clashed there, right before a big ASEAN (Southeast Asian nations) summit in Manila. 🚢⚠️

🌍 WORLD: Didn't expect this one -- Australia's alpine resorts just smashed July heat records, up to 12C above average, right in the middle of ski season. Hotham, Falls Creek and Thredbo all set new highs. 🏔️🌡️

🌍 WORLD: Trump's tariff fight with Canada just escalated hard -- he's defending a new 50% tariff on most Canadian goods, saying Canada "can't survive" without a deal. Carney says he'll intensify talks with the US.

🌍 WORLD: This one's quietly serious -- Ukraine's president Zelensky is facing growing protests at home, with demonstrators giving him until Friday to reinstate his fired defence minister and remove the military chief.
🌍 WORLD: Didn't expect this detail -- a Spanish hotel chain, Meliá, says it's pulling out of Cuba completely, shutting all 34 of its properties there because of US sanctions pressure.

🌍 WORLD: Zelensky just made a major move -- he's fired Ukraine's top army commander Oleksandr Syrskyi after days of public protests over the defence minister's ouster.

This is the military leadership shakeup protesters demanded, and it's happening mid-war.

🌍 WORLD: The Iran conflict keeps escalating and it's getting expensive and dangerous fast -- Hegseth says the war has cost the US $37.5 billion so far, and Trump says strikes on Iran aren't 'finished.'

Trump's now naming a specific next target too.

🌍 WORLD: Spain's got a serious wildfire on its hands -- a major blaze north of Madrid has forced about 1,200 people out and already burned over 30,000 hectares.

Officials are calling it one of the most complex fires the country's faced in years.

🌍 WORLD: Interesting one out of France -- it just became the first European country to ban social media for anyone under 15, passed by both its Senate and National Assembly.

Only the second country in the world to do this, after Australia. Here's the context:

🌍 WORLD: Sobering number out of the Iran conflict -- Hegseth says the war has now cost the US $37.5 billion, and it's the 11th straight night of American strikes with no diplomacy in sight.

Trump says the US has 'no interest' in talks right now.

🌍 WORLD: Watching this one closely -- a fast-moving wildfire in southern France burned through 1,700 hectares in just a few hours, forcing hundreds to evacuate.

Nearly 1,000 firefighters and dozens of aircraft are on it. The context:

🌍 WORLD: Zelensky just fired his top general too -- Oleksandr Syrskyi is out, days after protests erupted over the defence minister's removal.

This is the second big military leadership change in a week, mid-war, driven by street pressure. The context:
🌍 WORLD: Didn't expect a food-safety case to end in a possible prison sentence -- prosecutors in South Korea want a year in jail for a Michelin-starred chef who served ants in a dessert.
🌍 WORLD: Big one for parents to know about -- France's parliament has now formally passed its ban on social media for kids under 15, taking effect after summer break.
